chapters.title: Your mindset defines your life path
chapters.text:
Playing poker carries a certain stigma in society. People automatically assume that it is a synonym for gambling — a risky and questionable craft that promises more chances of losing than winning. However, the only thing that connects these two concepts is betting. Both in poker and gambling, you have to risk a certain amount of money on the game's outcome. However, only the best cards can yield the desired results in gambling. Poker players never know who is going to win.

Poker is so unpredictable that even the player with the worst cards can win.

For the uninitiated person, the poker game might seem effortless — just like in the movies. The mysterious central character pulls out the cards and gets all the money. In reality, millions of thoughts race through the minds of poker players. They assess each possibility, try to scan opponents' demeanor, and base their decisions on their assumptions. Imagine the situation in a poker room. When the player gets their cards, they must decide which strategy to follow — to call or raise the bet or fold the hand (surrender the cards). Other participants must read these behavioral signals and calculate the odds — if the player has a good hand or is simply bluffing.

Placing bets on uncertainty is one of the main factors in playing poker. When decisions lead to bad outcomes in real life, people blame themselves for their wrong choices. By playing the poker game, individuals can see this issue through the lens of probability — the world is unpredictable, and even the most feasible events may change their course. People have to adapt to this fluidity of things. Nate Silver, one of the topmost political analysts in the U.S., predicted that Hillary Clinton had a 71% chance of winning the 2016 election. Everyone assumed Clinton was going to win. No media outlet considered the enormity of the 29% chance that Trump had of winning. People misinterpreted 71% as 100% and made a mistake.

Did you know? The statistics conducted during six months show that poker players with excellent hands (in other words, cards they received from the dealer) won only 12 percent of the time.

chapters.title: Poker reflects on the whimsical nature of life
chapters.text:

chapters.title: Let the defeat be your mentor
chapters.text:
The scientist from Harvard, Ellen Langer, conducted an experiment to display how success can impair objective thinking. She asked her students to foresee the results of the coin toss. The professor prearranged whether people would get heads or tails. Some students got more guesses at the beginning of the experiment, while others could get the correct answers at the end of the trial. After the investigation, Langer asked students whether they had a flair for this activity. Those from the group who succeeded in the second part of the experiment responded negatively. They accepted that this was a game of chance. Remarkably, the other half gained confidence from their triumph and answered with “yes” — they assumed they could control the activity, which depended purely on luck.

Suppose your initial experience in a new venture is an absolute success. In that case, it becomes almost impossible to assess if you are indeed that brilliant or if it was a case of being serendipitously lucky. The same might happen to novices in the poker game. Poker is about being at ease with unpredictability without focusing on the results and the proper course of action. If people win at the beginning of their careers, they get incredibly delusional about their abilities. This self-confidence and ego can be indicators of future failure.

On the contrary, defeats are the best teachers. Losses demonstrate that you still have room for improvement; they stimulate you to keep growing professionally and look for new strategies and tactics. Sharpen your analytical and self-evaluation abilities so that you can continuously examine your current position and decide whether you're skilled enough to keep playing.

Open-mindedness is the key to success — be curious about everything and learn from your mistakes.

Many individuals often believe life is unfair to them when things don't go their way, but this isn't always true. People often don't learn from their mistakes and take a loss personally. Instead of reflecting on their actions to determine where things might have gone wrong, they accuse others.

Learning to lose in a game — to lose constructively and productively — would help me lose in life, lose and come back, lose and not see it as a personal failure. ~ Maria Konnikova

chapters.title: Don’t be afraid to make your move
chapters.text:
In poker, it’s best to act like a dragonfly — a superior hunter that most people hardly consider. Despite their small size and seemingly harmless looks, these insects capture over 90% of their targeted prey. If you judge its ability by its size, you'll overlook the efficacy of a dragonfly. Unlike wolves, which capture around 14% of their targets, dragonflies know when to strike and how to attack. The eyes of dragonflies can spot the slightest movement; their wings make it possible to maneuver with light-speed reflexes. Their brains have also evolved to anticipate precisely the potential changes to the target's movement. Dragonflies are so good at hunting because of their acute sight and the predictability of motion that their brains provide.

Some athletes have achieved greatness due to their trademark playing style. However, it would be best for the poker enthusiast to exercise freedom of thought. You must adopt a different approach whenever a new player joins the game or if you swap places to remain in the game. Then, be prepared to take any action necessary to win in the given situation. Much of poker relies more on experience than it does on memory. You learn what a good hand is by making a bunch of mistakes.

In poker, avoid sticking to a pre-planned strategy — that makes you predictable.

Challenge yourself to be active. The fear of failure makes many play poker passively. Think about the instances where you remove yourself from competitive settings due to another person's bravery when you clearly shouldn't. Consider how often you could have effectively taken charge and changed a scenario but instead chose to remain inactive and allow it to get the best of you. With the right cards, even an amateur can win a poker game. However, the chances of getting the best hand are slim, so if you keep waiting for this opportunity, your advantage will eventually run out.

Gender roles play a significant role in passivity. Most poker players are male. Due to the common misconception, women can only observe rather than have a seat at the table. Even when females tone up their aggression level just to fit in, this attitude puts some men off, and it causes an imbalance. Male players underestimate women in poker; if you are a woman, take advantage of this thinking pattern to make intelligent moves and outplay your opponents.

chapters.title: Your mindset defines your life path
chapters.text:
Your interpretation of events and experiences can significantly influence the outcome. Also, the words and expressions you use to describe yourself matter. Do you see yourself as a loser or as a winner? The former usually laments that the decks don’t favor them. They believe they are just in the middle of things and cannot turn the tide. A negative perception of yourself inevitably leads to self-sabotage — you strongly believe in your defeat and give up on other things you might try. Being pessimistic harms the game’s results and the person's mental health — it infects the mind. Besides, nagging and victimization irritate other people.

Stop fixating on the negatives; instead, try to come up with the best solution to the problem.

Contrastingly, the person with the mindset of a winner takes advantage even of the worst hand possible. Regardless of the result, the victor sees other possibilities and believes everything will work out — they will persistently get through tricky moments and get ready to seize the next opportunity. A close analogy is a choice between half-full and half-empty glasses — perspective here is the key.

The true winners are level-headed and rational. They understand that there are limits to their involvement in poker. The mecca of the gambling world, Las Vegas, offers various casinos. These establishments are all alluring and bright, with alcohol and other freebies. But there is a dark side to visiting casinos — they aim to make customers stay as long as possible. Resisting this temptation and not stepping over the boundaries of spending money and time takes a lot of effort and perseverance.

Your lucky strike might last for weeks, months, or even years. An exceptional situation happened with a German player Fedor Holz who experienced a hot streak in the tournaments. His results were outstanding, and someone calculated if this tendency might repeat. According to the statistics, the chances of someone else replicating Holz's success were less than 1%. Therefore, it would help if you prepared for the worst-case scenario — always get a plan B in case of money loss. This cautiousness is a beneficial quality not only in poker; it is a life-essential feature.

chapters.title: Stop being an unlucky person
chapters.text:
French chemist Louis Pasteur is the author of one of the most famous sayings about luck — “Chance favors the prepared mind.” People, however, frequently overlook that some part of the quote is missing. In reality, Pasteur claimed that chance only benefits the ready mind when it is paying attention. Individuals often emphasize mental readiness, but they must understand that without observation, preparation is worthless.

In a classic experiment, renowned psychologist Richard Wiseman asked participants to read a newspaper and count the number of photographs. Two categories of people took part in this test — those who considered themselves lucky and those who didn't. The first group needed only a few seconds to complete the task. The second group took around 120 seconds to count. Of course, this test had a tricky part — you could find a phrase stating the number of pictures, and the first category of participants managed to notice it.

In an age of constant distraction and never-ending connectivity, we may be so busy that we miss the signals that tell us to swerve before we’re in the bad beat’s path. ~ Maria Konnikova

This experiment supports the theory that people who see themselves as lucky can spot and take advantage of the opportunity. They are more attentive to their surroundings. To succeed, people must develop their awareness skills, be alert to the unpredictable, and carefully study every hint that luck offers. Of course, humans have no control over fortune. However, they are in charge of their concentration levels.

Some poker fans see the game as the energy flow between players. There is no plan or strategy — you must pay attention to what is happening at the table. Do your opponents look anxious? Do they act in a more unpredictable way than they used to before? Players exchange their energies, and those who find a happy medium in their expressions and movements can win. If you lose the game, this failure can make you rethink your strategy, and with the new style, you might obtain your victory. In other cases, the loss can demonstrate that poker is not your calling; you must focus on other things.

Poker is an excellent imitation of life that teaches us the importance of taking chances and seeing immediate results.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Many people will argue that playing poker is not enough to grasp the essence of uncertainty. Real life is much more complicated and unpredictable; there are things you cannot change with mathematical and strategic calculations or complete peace of mind. Those are critical health conditions or natural catastrophes. Indeed, poker is a simplified version of reality, but it still can teach techniques to face chaos and instability.

Poker shows the truth of life — people cannot see the future and gain complete control of the situation. They cannot modify the chain of events that emerged from pure luck. Take a look at your own life. There is a touch of chance even in your birth — your parents could’ve never met and got married, and they could’ve decided not to have kids. And now you are sitting here, chosen among thousands and millions of probabilities.

There is immense strength and willpower in accepting things as they are; obtaining this quality brings wisdom. Even if you face adversities, consider them necessary and inevitable — they might change your life completely (hopefully, in the best way possible), or you might learn crucial lessons from them. For example, losing the job might signify that something better awaits you — a new position, company, or even a completely different activity. Don't fixate on the negative side; move on with the new mindset.

Try this

If you want to get into poker, watch videos on Youtube to see how the game works. Afterward, you may proceed with online poker and test your understanding of rules and skills.
